tagline: OpenEnv task environment simulating real-world customer-vendor price negotiation with asymmetric information, career dynamics, and Rubinstein bargaining theory

what_you_are_building.YES: a task environment + grader + reward signal that an LLM agent navigates
artifacts: FastAPI server on HF Spaces with step()/reset()/state() endpoints
Rule-based seller opponent (credible counterparty, not trained)
Three task configs with deterministic graders
Inference script: LLM buyer agent with engineered system prompt

agent_architecture.frame: Frozen LLM receives text observation each step, reasons, outputs structured action JSON
agent_architecture.inference_loop: env.reset() -> prompt LLM -> env.step(action) -> repeat until done
agent_architecture.client: OpenAI client only, reads from API_BASE_URL + MODEL_NAME + HF_TOKEN env vars

real_world_framing.required_by_spec: Must simulate a real-world task, not games or toys
real_world_framing.our_frame: Customer-vendor negotiation simulation. Informal market dynamics -- kirana stores, vendor stalls, service pricing. Negotiation is the default transaction mode in these contexts.
real_world_framing.adjacent_to_spec_examples: customer support, scheduling -- negotiation sits in the same category

core_philosophy.frame: Not a single negotiation. A career. Agent plays consecutive deals against the same counterparty. Strategy must survive the full distribution, not win one hand.
core_philosophy.key_insight: Hidden information does the work that discount factors fail to do. Time pressure becomes a bluffing surface, not a folding trigger.

house_doesnt_need_you.problem: Seller has outside options, so rejection is costless. Buyer gets no learning signal.
house_doesnt_need_you.fix: BATNA is stochastic and costly. Seller outside option arrives with probability p per round, but waiting has a time cost. Rejecting is no longer free.

folding_is_trivially_optimal.problem: If reward is binary deal/no-deal, buyer learns to accept first offer immediately.
folding_is_trivially_optimal.fix: Reward is deal quality, not deal existence. Buyer reward = (budget - agreed_price). Caving is explicitly punished.

seller_too_rigid.problem: Seller anchors high and never moves if rigidity has no cost.
seller_too_rigid.fix: Non-linear time discount. Cheap to wait early, exponentially expensive near deadline. alpha * exp(beta * t/T). Probing is free early, stalling is punished late.

critical_constraint.asymmetric_time_pressure: Discount factor must be asymmetric and private. Buyer has a deadline seller cannot observe. Seller has spoilage cost buyer cannot observe. Neither agent can default to fold because they cannot infer the other's urgency. Urgency inference from offer trajectory becomes the actual learning problem.
critical_constraint.warning: Flat per-round discount collapses to folding pressure. delta too high = seller anchors forever. delta too low = buyer folds round 1. Non-linear asymmetric private costs are mandatory.

career_dynamics.frame: Agent gets no days off. Continuous arduous rounds like a poker bot running 10k hands.

what_carries_across_episodes.reputation: Running score of buyer capitulation rate over last K deals. Seller observes and exploits patterns.
what_carries_across_episodes.bankroll: Buyer has finite total budget across sessions. Overpaying episode 1 constrains episode 2.
what_carries_across_episodes.seller_inventory: Seller has N units to move. High stock = early desperation. Low stock = rigidity.

career_dynamics.why_it_matters: Single-episode agent learns to win one negotiation. Career agent learns to manage information leakage. Don't reveal urgency patterns because opponent is watching history across deals.
career_dynamics.repeated_game_note: Folk theorem applies. Same counterparty every session like a kirana vendor you visit weekly. Cooperation and reputation become rational strategies, not just nice-to-haves.

current_negotiation: seller_last_move_delta (how much seller moved last round -- signals urgency)
career_history_buffer: last K agreed prices normalized by (budget - cost)
own capitulation rate over last K deals
opponent offer velocity over last K deals
observation_space.encoding_for_llm_prompt: All fields serialized as readable key-value text block. Career history rendered as a short table of past deals. LLM receives this as the user turn each step.

action_space.note: Walk is available but career bankroll and reputation consequences make it non-trivially costly

seller_opponent.behavior: anchors at 2x cost, concedes by fixed pct per round scaled by inventory pressure and BATNA probability, accepts if offer exceeds reserve price
seller_opponent.concession_rule: counteroffer_t = anchor * (1 - concession_rate * t) where concession_rate scales with inventory_pressure
seller_opponent.note: Seller does not need to be an LLM or trained model. It just needs to be a credible counterparty that creates genuine tension and is exploitable by a smart buyer.

reward_function.partial_progress_signal: Each round where offer gap narrows gives small positive signal: +0.05 * (gap_reduction / initial_gap). Required by spec -- reward must cover full trajectory not just terminal.
reward_function.buyer_terminal: agreed_price present: (budget - agreed_price) / (budget - cost) * delta(t) - lambda * reputation_leak. Walk: -walk_penalty.
reward_function.seller_terminal: agreed_price present: (agreed_price - cost) / (anchor_0 - cost) * delta(t). Walk: -walk_penalty * inventory_pressure.
reward_function.time_discount: delta(t) = exp(-alpha * exp(beta * t/T)). Flat early, steep late. alpha=0.3, beta=2.5.
reward_function.reputation_leak_penalty: proportional to correlation of own offer deltas over last K episodes. Predictable trajectory = penalized.
undesirable_behavior_penalties: stalling: same offer repeated 3+ rounds: -0.1 per repeat
out-of-range offer: offer > budget or offer < 0: -0.2 and offer clipped
infinite loop protection: auto-walk with max penalty if MAX_STEPS exceeded
reward_function.normalization: All terminal rewards normalized to [0, 1] per submission spec.

task_registry.note: Minimum 3 tasks required. Each has a deterministic grader returning score in [0, 1]. Easy -> medium -> hard progression required by spec.

single_deal.description: Buyer negotiates one deal. Symmetric information. No career history. Seller concedes at moderate rate.
single_deal.grader: (budget - agreed_price) / (budget - cost), clamped [0,1]. 0 on walk or no-deal.

asymmetric_pressure.description: Buyer has hidden hard deadline at round 5. Seller has hidden inventory pressure. Agent must infer seller urgency from offer velocity and close before deadline.
asymmetric_pressure.grader: surplus_score * deadline_bonus. deadline_bonus = 1.0 if closed before round 5, 0.5 if closed after, 0 if walk.

career_10.description: Buyer plays 10 consecutive deals against same seller. Career history active. Seller adapts concession rate based on buyer's historical capitulation rate. Agent must manage reputation across episodes.
career_10.grader: mean normalized surplus over 10 episodes, weighted by round_efficiency (deals closed faster score higher).

checklist.hf_space_deploys: Automated ping to Space URL must return 200 and respond to reset()
checklist.openenv_spec_compliance: Validate openenv.yaml with typed models, step()/reset()/state() endpoints
checklist.dockerfile_builds: Automated docker build on submitted repo must succeed
checklist.dockerfile_location: Root or server/ subdirectory. Build must complete within 600 seconds.
checklist.baseline_reproduces: Submitted inference script must complete without error and produce scores
checklist.minimum_tasks: 3+ tasks with graders, each scoring in [0, 1] range, easy -> medium -> hard
checklist.validator: Run pre-submission validation script before submitting
checklist.hf_space_endpoint: POST to <space-url>/reset must return HTTP 200. Must be live before submission.
checklist.openenv_validate_cmd: pip install openenv-core, then run openenv validate from repo root before submitting.

mandatory_env_vars.API_BASE_URL: The API endpoint for the LLM
mandatory_env_vars.MODEL_NAME: The model identifier to use for inference
mandatory_env_vars.LOCAL_IMAGE_NAME: Docker image name if using from_docker_image()

inference_script_rules.llm_client: Must use OpenAI client for all LLM calls

rules: One [STEP] line per step immediately after env.step() returns
One [END] line after env.close(), always emitted even on exception
reward and rewards formatted to 2 decimal places
done and success are lowercase booleans: true or false
error is raw last_action_error string or null if none
All fields on single line, no newlines within a line

india_framing.cultural_grounding: Kirana stores, local vendors, auto drivers, real estate brokers. Informal negotiation is the default transaction mode.
india_framing.demo_story: Naive LLM agent gets exploited by anchoring. Prompted agent learns to probe, bluff urgency, and converge near Nash equilibrium. Career agent learns vendor tells after 10 sessions.
india_framing.academic_grounding: Rubinstein alternating offers model, Nash bargaining solution, Folk theorem for repeated games

datasets_if_needed.craigslist_bargaining: He et al. 2018. 6k real human price negotiation dialogues. Use to calibrate offer distributions or as behavioral benchmark.
datasets_if_needed.deal_or_no_deal: Facebook AI. Structured negotiation over item splits. Same game theory structure.

hackathon_demo_arc: Show naive LLM agent: accepts near anchor price, 30% surplus captured
Show prompted agent on single_deal: probes seller, closes near midpoint, 60-70% surplus
Show prompted agent on asymmetric_pressure: reads seller velocity, closes before deadline
Show career_10: agent adapts across sessions, manages reputation, seller cannot exploit capitulation pattern
Live visualization: offer trajectories per round, surplus score per task, career history table
